subject: mathematics
chapter_title: Orienting Yourself: The Use of Coordinates

core_competencies.justification: The whole chapter is built around specifying locations and describing spatial relationships using coordinate geometry: it defines the x-axis, y-axis, origin and the four quadrants, plots and reads off the coordinates of points such as B(4.5,0), G(0,-4.5) and H(0,4), and applies this throughout the Reiaan's-room exercises (Exercise Set 1.1, Exercise Set 1.2) to locate doors, furniture, and room corners by their coordinates.

adjunct_competencies.justification: Several exercises model daily-life situations through coordinate representations — the city street-grid intersection problem (End of Chapter Q14) and the computer-graphics screen with circular icons (End of Chapter Q15) both require translating a real-world layout into coordinates and drawing conclusions from that representation.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to specify the location of a point and describe spatial relationships between points by introducing the Cartesian coordinate system, its axes and quadrants, and the coordinate-based distance formula derived from the Baudhayana-Pythagoras theorem.
